topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge is a collection of various materials and tools used in System and Network administration, DevOps, Pentesting, and Security Research.

entries.q: Who is The Book of Secret Knowledge aimed at?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge is aimed at System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: What type of content can be found in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge contains a collection of inspiring lists, manuals, cheatsheets, blogs, hacks, one-liners, cli/web tools, and more.

entries.q: How can I contribute to The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: Contributions to The Book of Secret Knowledge can be made through pull requests, and should follow the contributing guidelines.

entries.q: What is the license for The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge is licensed under the MIT License.

entries.q: What are some of the topics covered in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: The Book of Secret Knowledge covers topics such as CLI Tools, GUI Tools, Web Tools, Systems/Services, Networks, Containers/Orchestration, and more.

entries.q: How can I stay updated with changes to The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: You can stay updated with changes to The Book of Secret Knowledge by subscribing to the RSS/Atom feed of the commits.

entries.q: What are some of the shell options mentioned in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: Some of the shell options mentioned in The Book of Secret Knowledge include GNU Bash, Zsh, tclsh, bash-it, Oh My ZSH!, and Oh My Fish.
